claim: Claude's context loss problem requires a multi-layer memory system: Claude.md for identity/rules, Obsidian for active reasoning/projects with graph relationships, and Pinecone for archived data and exact recall
actionable: Implement a three-layer memory: identity in Claude.md (read-first instructions, unchanging), active work in Obsidian (linked markdown, evolving), and static archives in Pinecone (transcripts, emails, PDFs).
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Obsidian LLM Wiki compounds knowledge over time: one new source updates 10-15 related wiki pages, while traditional RAG repeats the same query-retrieve-answer cycle without knowledge retention
actionable: Use Obsidian as a self-building wiki where Claude incrementally updates indexed pages with each new source added; set up an index.md and schema file to guide Claude's maintenance.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Obsidian RAG has limitations: token costs scale linearly with index size (750 tokens at 10 files, unbounded at 10,000 files), no semantic search (only topic-based), and it degrades with data drift and large datasets
actionable: Use Obsidian for small to medium projects (~100-1000 files); for larger datasets or transcripts, move to Pinecone to avoid exponential token growth and enjoy flat-cost semantic search.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Pinecone embedding ingestion is 100x cheaper than Obsidian because it runs embedding once and stores vectors, whereas Obsidian re-reads and re-processes entire context windows for each query
actionable: Move static archives (YouTube transcripts, PDFs, emails) to Pinecone; keep only active/changing content in Obsidian to balance cost and reasoning quality.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Obsidian RAG works best for structured reasoning and iterative refinement; Pinecone works best for perfect recall and similarity search; they serve different physics and should not compete
actionable: Think of Obsidian as 'how I think' (reasoning layer), Claude.md as 'who I am' (identity layer), and Pinecone as 'what I've said' (archive layer); choose each based on access patterns.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Obsidian web clipper can be customized to download images locally (raw assets folder) and attach them to notes, making research materials self-contained and portable
actionable: In Obsidian settings, set attachment folder path to fixed directory (e.g., raw assets), then use Hot Keys > Download Attachments to save images locally when clipping web content.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Linting in Obsidian RAG means periodically running a process to identify contradictions, stale claims, and maintenance tasks that humans would naturally forget in the knowledge base
actionable: Set up a monthly or quarterly linting process where Claude reviews the entire wiki for contradictions, outdated facts, and broken links; prompt it to flag items for human review.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Obsidian RAG requires three core pieces: raw sources folder, wiki folder (Claude-maintained markdown), and schema/rules file that tells Claude how to maintain the system
actionable: Create three folders: 'raw' for ingested sources, 'wiki' for Claude-generated summaries, and a rules file (rulebook) defining conventions and workflows for Claude to follow.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Graph view in Obsidian is primarily visual; it looks impressive but doesn't drive decision-making; the real value comes from understanding relationships between pages for reasoning
actionable: Don't rely on graph view to guide your system design; focus instead on the actual wiki links and page relationships that matter for reasoning about your domain.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta

claim: Pick one domain per Obsidian wiki (e.g., 'growing LinkedIn' or 'personal life decisions'); focus increases quality and compounds knowledge faster than multi-domain wikis
actionable: Create separate mini-wikis for distinct projects or domains; each wiki maintains its own index and schema; combine only when reasoning across domains is needed.
source_title: Claude Code + Karpathy's Obsidian = New Meta
